summary:
You start with a tree of n nodes labeled 1 to n, then one extra edge is added between two existing nodes, creating exactly one cycle. You are given the resulting list of edges.

Return the one edge that can be removed so the graph becomes a tree again. If more than one edge could be removed, return the one that appears last in the input list.

examples.explanation: Edges 1-2, 1-3, and 2-3 form a triangle; removing the last one, [2,3], leaves a valid tree.
The cycle is 1-2-3-4-1; [1,4] is the last edge of that cycle in the list, so it is removed.

constraints: Each edge connects two distinct nodes labeled 1 to n
The input forms a tree plus exactly one additional edge (one cycle)
If several answers are possible, return the edge that occurs last in the list
